**About Department**

The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. Initially, the department was maintained by the faculty members of IIT Delhi. From 2017 onwards new faculty members started to join the department and till now the department has four faculty members and two more will be joining soon. In addition to faculty members, the department has one Women Scientist. Currently the department has one technical staff. The department is contributing to the undergraduate courses related to the B.Tech. program. The department is also running PhD program in Chemistry, which was started from July 2018. At present, the department has a total of seven PhD students including externally funded students from agencies like CSIR-UGC and DST INSPIRE. In addition, the department has one Junior Research Fellow funded by DST SERB.

Thrust areas of research of the department include asymmetric synthesis, hypervalent iodine chemistry, bio-mimetic and sustainable catalysis, organometallic chemistry, computational chemistry (photocatalytic water splitting), advanced transition-metal chemistry, colloids and surfactant chemistry. For research activities, the department is already well equipped with laboratories having several sophisticated analytical equipment and high configuration computer facilities. The department has a total of five externally funded research projects worth of approximately 1.6 crores from external funding agencies like DST.

### Message

Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. This webpage provides all the essential information about the department. In our department, teaching and research are synergistic to each other. Our research interests span various major areas of chemical sciences, ranging from synthetic organic and inorganic chemistry to computational, physical, and material chemistry

Our group’s research primarily focuses on the design and development of new materials and their hybrids to address current challenges associated with the energy crisis and environmental issues. The objective of this perspective is to delve into recent developments in emerging hybrid two-dimensional (2D) porous materials (H2DPMs), utilizing various 2D materials in conjunction with nanoporous materials through primary or secondary bonding interactions. We aim to highlight recent advances in synthesis strategies, characterization techniques, and the progress made in energy storage/conversion as well as environmental and industrial applications.

ChemistryAvinash Dhamija

### **Avinash Dhamija**

Ramanujan Faculty

#### Brief Info

Dr. Avinash Dhamija joined the Department of Chemistry at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu as a Ramanujan Fellow in February 2026. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was awarded the prestigious Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowship for his project on “Microfluidic Diffusion-Based Chiral Separation.” The fellowship was carried out in two phases: first at the Supramolecular Science and Engineering Institute, founded by Nobel Laureate Jean-Marie Lehn, and later at IMDEA Nanociencia, Spain (2023–2025). Earlier, Dr. Dhamija was awarded the Center for Self-Assembly and Complexity Fellowship, under which he conducted research at the Institute for Basic Science, POSTECH, from 2019 to 2022. His research focuses on advanced supramolecular systems, self-assembly, and microfluidic approaches for chiral separation.

Throughout his academic career, Dr. Dhamija has received several prestigious awards and fellowships in recognition of his research excellence. These include the CSC Best Researcher Award from the Institute for Basic Science, the Lectureship Award from POSTECH, and the Best Presentation Award from the Korean Chemical Society. He has also been honored with highly competitive fellowships, including the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellowship funded by the European Horizon Programme, the CSC Fellowship from IBS, and the Ramanujan Fellowship awarded by the Anusandhan National Research Foundation.

#### Research Interest

Dr. Avinash Dhamija’s research focuses on understanding the structure–function relationships of self-assembled molecular systems and utilizing these insights for the rational design of smart functional materials.

Key research interests include:

- Exploring supramolecular self-assembly for advanced material development.
- Developing porous organic cages for functional applications.
- Developing organic photovoltaic materials for sustainable energy conversion and next-generation optoelectronic devices.
- Controlling molecular assembly processes using flow chemistry/microfluidic platforms for efficient and scalable material synthesis.

### Curriculum Development and Programme Leadership

During my tenure as Head of the Department of Chemistry at IIT Jammu (2020–2022), I had the privilege of initiating and leading the establishment of the M.Sc. and MS(R) programmes in Chemistry.

#### Research Experience

Our group’s research primarily focuses on the design and development of new materials and their hybrids to address current challenges associated with the energy crisis and environmental issues. The objective of this perspective is to delve into recent developments in emerging hybrid two-dimensional (2D) porous materials (H2DPMs), utilizing various 2D materials in conjunction with nanoporous materials through primary or secondary bonding interactions. We aim to highlight recent advances in synthesis strategies, characterization techniques, and the progress made in energy storage/conversion as well as environmental and industrial applications.

#### Joint Research Work

In addition to foundational materials development, our research group is deeply committed to interdisciplinary collaboration and product-oriented innovation. We actively pursue translational research by integrating advanced materials into practical systems with broad societal impact. Key collaborative efforts span across multiple engineering disciplines, including:

- Mechanical Engineering – for the development of flexible, stretchable, and mechanically robust devices.
- Materials Engineering – for composite design, structural integration, and materials optimization.
- Civil Engineering – for designing geopolymer concrete systems embedded with nanomaterials for self-healing, pollutant capture, and enhanced durability.
- Electrical Engineering – for designing functional sensors and incorporating advanced materials into IoT-based platforms and energy-harvesting systems.
- Environmental Engineering – for atmospheric water harvesting using functional porous frameworks and membrane-based systems.
- Chemical Engineering – for electro-photo-thermal CO₂ reduction by integrating hybrid catalysts into scalable, sustainable energy platforms.

Our research is inherently translational, targeting industry-relevant solutions through the convergence of materials science, engineering design, and device prototyping. This interdisciplinary strategy enables the practical deployment of next-generation materials, bridging the gap between academic discovery and real-world technology.

ChemistryShirsendu Ghosh

### **Shirsendu Ghosh**

Assistant Professor

#### Brief Info

Dr. Shirsendu Ghosh did his graduation from Jadavpur University with honors in Chemistry (2009). Then he obtained his master’s degree in Chemistry from IIT Kharagpur (2011). He received his Ph.D. degree from the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science (IACS), Kolkata in 2016. After a Postdoctoral stint at Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel (2016-2021) he joined as a research associate at Cornell University, USA (2021-2022). After that, he served as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry, GITAM Hyderabad for two years (2022-2024). Then he joined as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry with a joint affiliation in the Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu on December 2024. His area of research revolves around the development and use of new optical spectroscopy and microscopy techniques to decipher the dynamics of biological systems and live cells. More specifically, he developed total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy-based super-resolution microscopic techniques to investigate the role of plasma membrane topography in cancer biology and immunology (such as T-cell signaling pathways and Mast cell signaling pathways). He is also interested in deciphering the consequences of long-range charge reorganization within a protein on the allosteric signaling of proteins which may lead to the development of a pathway to control allosteric signaling. He employs single-molecule fluorescence microscopy and time-resolved confocal microscopy techniques to decipher the changes in polarity, viscosity, and dynamics of different regions of biomolecules (such as proteins, DNA, lipid vesicles, etc.) and live cells. He is also interested in the development of low-cost protein-coated copper nanoclusters for drug delivery and ion sensing. He is the recipient of the prestigious Ramanujan Fellowship (SERB), DST-inspired faculty fellowship, Koshland Prize (Koshland Foundation, Israel), and CSIR-Research Fellowship.

**PhD Position Available in Single Molecule Biophysics Lab**

Join Our Cutting-Edge Research Team!

Are you passionate about pushing the boundaries of science? Do you want to explore the complexities of biological processes at the molecular level? We are looking for a highly motivated PhD student to join our dynamic research group in Single Molecule Biophysics.

**Project Overview:** In this project, you will use advanced biophysical techniques to study the behavior of individual molecules, providing unprecedented insight into cellular processes. You will explore topics such as membrane biophysics, specifically membrane topography and domains, immunological signaling, protein folding, allosteric signaling in proteins, DNA/RNA interactions with small molecules, and other key biological mechanisms in health and disease.

## Dr. Nityananda Sahu

#### Message

Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. This webpage provides all the essential information about the department. In our department, teaching and research are synergistic to each other. Our research interests span various major areas of chemical sciences, ranging from synthetic organic and inorganic chemistry to computational, physical, and material chemistry. The department strives to create an environment that fosters curiosity-driven research and high-quality education. We are committed to nurturing the next generation of scientists and engineers, equipping them with the skills necessary to make meaningful contributions to science and society.
